2605.02395.title: Controllable and Verifiable Process Data Synthesis for Process Reward Models
2605.02395.paper_summary: Process reward models (PRMs) rely on high-quality process supervision data, yet existing construction methods often provide limited control over error location, error type, and trajectory consistency. We propose a controllable and verifiable framework for synthesizing process supervision data for PRMs. Our framework first constructs a correct symbolic reasoning chain, injects a template-aware error into an intermediate step, recomputes subsequent steps under the corrupted state, and verifies that the injected step is not derivable from its prefix. The resulting paired trajectories are prefix-invalid at the first error while remaining trajectory-consistent after symbolic recomputation, and are translated into aligned natural-language processes for PRM training and evaluation. Experiments show that the synthesized data improve Best-of-8 reranking on logical reasoning benchmarks and transfer to mathematical reasoning. Step-level evaluation further shows that first-error localization remains substantially more challenging than overall step classification, highlighting the need for fine-grained and verifiable process supervision.
2605.02395.paper_summary_zh: 過程獎勵模型（PRMs）依賴於高質量的過程監督數據，然而現有的構建方法往往對錯誤位置、錯誤類型和軌跡一致性提供有限的控制。我們提出了一個可控且可驗證的框架，用於合成PRMs的過程監督數據。我們的框架首先構建一個正確的符號推理鏈，將一個模板感知的錯誤注入到中間步驟中，在損壞狀態下重新計算後續步驟，並驗證注入的步驟無法從其前綴推導出來。所得到的配對軌跡在第一次錯誤時是前綴無效的，同時在符號重新計算後保持軌跡一致性，並被轉換為對齊的自然語言過程，用於PRM的訓練和評估。實驗顯示，合成的數據提高了邏輯推理基準上的最佳8重排效果，並可轉移到數學推理上。步驟級別的評估進一步顯示，第一次錯誤定位仍然比整體步驟分類更具挑戰性，突顯了對細粒度和可驗證的過程監督的需求。
2605.02395.author: Yinghui Chi et.al.
